channel.title: Federico Bruzzone
channel.description: Posts, scientific publications, preprints, and activities by Federico Bruzzone.

item.title: The Site Now Has an RSS Feed
item.description: The website now has an RSS feed that gathers posts, scientific publications, preprints, and activities into one chronological stream. Subscribe with your favourite reader at /feed.xml.

item.title: Regime-Based Capability Graph Semantics (RCGS)
item.description: TODO

item.title: A Friendly Tour of Substructural, Uniqueness, Ownership, and Capabilities Types — and more!
item.description: The third post of the Eter programming language series. A friendly tour of the type-theoretic landscape behind memory safety. Starting from the logical roots of substructural logic, it walks through linear, affine, and uniqueness types, then visits regions, effects, capabilities, typestate, and the latest work on reachability and separation types.

item.title: Mutable Value Semantics (MVS) or Ownership & Borrowing: A Trade-off Analysis
item.description: The second post of the Eter programming language series. It explores the trade-offs between Mutable Value Semantics and Ownership & Borrowing, examining friction points in Rust, Hylo, and Swift while searching for common ground between the two memory models.

item.title: Generalized Software Product Line Extraction
item.description: F. Bruzzone, W. Cazzola, and L. Favalli — arXiv preprint arXiv:2605.28989 — DOI: 10.48550/arXiv.2605.28989

item.title: The Mutable Value Semantics (MVS): A Non-superficial Study
item.description: The first post of the Eter programming language series. While this post is part of the series, it is not a part of the Eter language itself. The post is a non-superficial study on the MVS, trying to understand its limitations.

item.title: Sink or SWIM: Tackling Real-Time ASR at Scale
item.description: F. Bruzzone, W. Cazzola, M. Brancaleoni, and D. Pellegrino — IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing (TASLPRO) — DOI: 10.1109/TASLPRO.2026.3675780

item.title: Meta-Monomorphizing Specializations
item.description: F. Bruzzone and W. Cazzola — arXiv preprint arXiv:2602.12973 — DOI: 10.48550/arXiv.2602.12973

item.title: From Separate Compilation to Sound Language Composition
item.description: F. Bruzzone, W. Cazzola, and L. Favalli — arXiv preprint arXiv:2602.03777 — DOI: 10.48550/arXiv.2602.03777

item.title: Prioritizing Configuration Relevance via Compiler-Based Refined Feature Ranking
item.description: F. Bruzzone, W. Cazzola, and L. Favalli — arXiv preprint arXiv:2601.16008 — DOI: 10.48550/arXiv.2601.16008

item.title: Code Less to Code More: Streamlining Language Server Protocol and type system development for language families
item.description: F. Bruzzone, W. Cazzola, and L. Favalli — Journal of Systems and Software (JSS) — DOI: 10.1016/j.jss.2025.112554

item.title: Consume the Input Only When Some<T> is Returned
item.description: A discussion on idiomatic Rust patterns for consuming input only when a function successfully returns a value. This explores ownership semantics and how to write clean, expressive APIs in Rust.

item.title: One of the 393 contributors to Rust 1.86.0
item.description: I am one of the 393 contributors to Rust 1.86.0. You can find the release notes here.

item.title: Open Source Licenses: A Deep Dive into Their Meaning
item.description: An in-depth exploration of open source licenses, covering their legal implications, the differences between permissive and copyleft licenses, and practical guidance for choosing the right license for your project.

item.title: Generalizing Closeness centrality to weighted networks using Newman method
item.description: This PR closed the issue #1384 by generalizing the Closeness centrality to weighted networks using the Newman method. It provides both the Rust implementation and the Python bindings.

item.title: Use the type-level constant value ty::Value where needed
item.description: With this PR, I refactored functions to accept a single type-level constant value <tt>ty::Value</tt> instead of separate <tt>ty::Valtree</tt> and <tt>ty::Ty</tt> parameters.

item.title: Add TooGeneric to LayoutError and emit Unknown
item.description: With this PR, I added the <tt>TooGeneric</tt> variant to the <tt>LayoutError</tt> enum to emit the error when a type is too generic to be laid out. The <tt>Unknown</tt> variant is emitted when the layout of a type is unknown.

item.title: One of the 396 contributors to Rust 1.83.0
item.description: I am one of the 396 contributors to Rust 1.83.0. You can find the release notes here.

item.title: 56th Top Github Contributor in Italy out of 958
item.description: I am the 56th top Github contributor in Italy out of 958 according to this classification.

item.title: First Contribution to Rust
item.description: After the study of the rustc-dev-guide, I made my first small contribution to the Rust compiler.

item.title: Student Volunteer at ICFP 2024
item.description: I was a student volunteer at the 29th ACM SIGPLAN International Conference on Functional Programming (ICFP 2024).

item.title: Exploiting Finite State Automata for Efficient Lexical Analysis: A Rust Implementation
item.description: A practical exploration of building a lexical analyzer in Rust using finite state automata. We cover DFA/NFA theory and show how to implement an efficient lexer from scratch.

item.title: Admission to the PhD
item.description: I was admitted to the PhD in Computer Science at the University of Milan with the project Universal Language Server Protocol and Debugger Adapter Protocol for Modular Language Workbenches under the supervision of Prof. W. Cazzola.

item.title: P4 Compiler in SDN
item.description: Presentation on the P4 compiler in Software Defined Networking (SDN)

item.title: PhD Project
item.description: Presentation of the PhD project Universal Language Server Protocol and Debugger Adapter Protocol for Modular Language Workbenches at the University of Milan.

item.title: Obtaining the Master's Degree
item.description: I graduated cum laude in Computer Science at the University of Milan with the thesis Toward a Modular Approach for Type Systems and LSP Generation under the supervision of Prof. W. Cazzola.

item.title: Master's Thesis
item.description: Presentation of the Master's Thesis Toward a Modular Approach for Type Systems and LSP Generation at the University of Milan.
